articles.title: UBS raises Brent forecast to $105, yet to see 'demand destruction'
articles.description: Giovanni Staunovo, commodity analyst at UBS Global Wealth Management, says the oil markets may not be accurately pricing in energy supply shortage and are instead driven by sentiment. He says demand hasn't come down enough to push oil prices lower. He also discusses how much time he expects it would take for oil production to pick up to pre-conflict levels.

He shares his outlook and investment strategy for Middle East equity markets, and highlights two stocks that investors can consider to capitalize on the growth opportunity in the region.

articles.title: Future of the Quad alliance is unclear, says Kugelman
articles.description: Michael Kugelman, Senior Fellow, South Asia, at the Atlantic Council says President Donald Trump might travel to India to attend the Quad Summit later in the year if he had something big to announce, such as the US-India trade deal. He also adds that such a visit by Trump would aid India-U.S. ties.

articles.title: SK Hynix joins the $1 trillion club: Is the AI boom moving to East Asia?
articles.description: Phillip Wool, Chief Research Officer at Rayliant Global Advisors, remains positive on SK Hynix, Samsung and TSMC. He adds that he sees investment opportunity among software stocks, which he thinks have been oversold over AI concerns.

articles.title: Everything AI: Economist on 'MSN' strategy driving China's industrial profits
articles.description: Hao Zhou of Guotai Junan International discusses the diverging indicators of the Chinese economy after April industrial profits delivered the fastest gain in over two years with a 24.7% jump. Zhou said the momentum is largely due to the "MSN" sectors - memory, semiconductor, and new energy - which are riding a massive wave of AI-driven demand. Meanwhile, traditional downstream industries like automakers and smartphone manufacturers are feeling the squeeze of rising upstream costs and shifting capital.

articles.title: A moment that changed me: I was turning 40 with an arthritis diagnosis – on a whim I took up my favourite teen hobby again
articles.description: I started kickboxing 20 years ago in a bid to be like Buffy the Vampire Slayer, but thought I could never manage all the punching and jumping. It turns out I could handle much more than I thought At 14, I decided to learn a martial art. I told my parents it was to defend myself on the mean streets of Congleton – a market town in Cheshire largely devoid of danger – when, in truth, it was because I wanted to be like Buffy the Vampire Slayer. I joined a kickboxing club, and what could have been a passing phase became a thrice-weekly commitment spanning four years. I was a model student, picking up a different coloured belt every few months to mark my progression through the grades. I grew strong and flexible, swapping puppy fat for muscle. I routinely fought men without fear and found a confidence in my body I have never experienced before or since. Continue reading...

articles.title: Cells detect and silence invading transposons through abnormal RNA signals
articles.description: Transposons, DNA sequences that can self-replicate and move (jump) throughout the genome, are widespread and can affect cell survival if left unchecked. Cells control these "jumping genes" by silencing them, but little was known about how cells recognize and defend themselves from invading transposons.

articles.title: Former AG Pam Bondi was diagnosed with cancer shortly after being axed by Trump: report
articles.description: Pam Bondi was diagnosed with thyroid cancer shortly after President Trump removed her as attorney general, according to a report Tuesday.

articles.title: ICU pneumonia mortality rates remain elevated in developing nations
articles.description: A scientific review published today in the NEJM Evidence journal, coordinated by the D'Or Institute for Research and Education (IDOR), evaluated outcomes of adults with community-acquired pneumonia (CAP) admitted to intensive care units (ICUs) in middle-income countries.

articles.title: Retatrutide reshapes metabolism in obesity and type 2 diabetes, study finds
articles.description: Retatrutide altered circulating metabolites and lipids linked to fatty acid oxidation and insulin resistance in people with obesity, with or without type 2 diabetes. These biomarker changes suggest improved metabolic health, but the post-hoc analysis remains hypothesis-generating and does not prove direct clinical benefit.

articles.title: China’s industrial maturity and supply chain control open exciting opportunities for Kuehne+Nagel

articles.title: The Trump Mental Health Issue We Don’t See: Biographer
articles.description: Photo Illustration by Thomas Levinson/The Daily Beast/Getty Donald Trump’s biographer says the aging president is suffering from loneliness and paranoia as the strains of his second term weigh on his mental and physical health. “He is paranoid, is angry ... things are not going well for him,” author Michael Wolff said on the Inside Trump’s Head podcast. “And what effect does that have on the overweight man who doesn’t exercise?” Wolff said the second term is a “downer” for any president: “You have nothing to work toward. You know, you’re not running again, you see your end, and that must be frightening. And also, everyone is taking things from you.” Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: SlimTide Capsules Exploding in 2026: Slim Tide SHOCKING Deep Story Behind the Viral Gut-Health Weight Management Trend!
articles.description: Lakeland, FL, May 26, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- In the crowded and highly competitive dietary supplement market, a new name has rapidly gained traction across wellness blogs, affiliate websites, social media advertisements, and online discussion forums: SlimTide. Transform your metabolism with SlimTide Visit Official Website Promoted as a "natural metabolism support formula" capable of enhancing fat burning, improving energy, suppressing cravings, and supporting mitochondrial health, SlimTide has become one of the most heavily marketed wellness supplements of 2026. Multiple websites describe it as a revolutionary approach to weight management, often targeting adults over 30 who struggle with stubborn fat, low energy, or slow metabolism. As the product's visibility increases, scrutiny has intensified around its legitimacy, scientific backing, ingredient transparency, and marketing practices. VISITING OFFICIAL WEBSITE OF SLIMTIDE An investigation into SlimTide reveals a fragmented ecosystem of promotional sites, conflicting product descriptions, recycled claims, and rising consumer skepticism. While some users report positive experiences, independent reviewers and consumer watchdogs have also raised concerns about exaggerated advertising and counterfeit listings. This article provides a detailed examination of what SlimTide is, how it is marketed, which ingredients are associated with the supplement, and whether the science behind its claims withstands scrutiny. What Is SlimTide? According to several official-style promotional websites, SlimTide is marketed as a natural dietary supplement formulated to support: Weight management Fat metabolism Appetite control Energy production Mitochondrial function Digestive or gut health Different SlimTide domains describe the product in slightly different ways. Some versions emphasize mitochondrial metabolism and fat burning, while others focus heavily on gut microbiome support using probiotics and resistant starches. One of the recurring themes in SlimTide marketing is the idea that traditional dieting fails because it does not address "cellular energy production." Several websites claim that impaired mitochondrial function contributes to sluggish metabolism and fat retention. Other promotional pages frame SlimTide as a gut-health solution, claiming that modulating intestinal microbiota composition can improve appetite regulation, digestive function, and metabolic performance. This dual branding strategy — combining metabolism support with gut-health optimization — appears central to the product's online positioning. The Ingredient Story: Multiple Formulas, Multiple Claims One of the most unusual aspects of SlimTide is the inconsistency between websites advertising the supplement. Some SlimTide pages describe a formula built around six "plant-based ingredients" intended to improve mitochondrial performance and energy production. Other reports and press releases identify a completely different formula consisting primarily of: Chicory Root Inulin Potato Resistant Starch A probiotic blend including: Bifidobacterium infantis Clostridium butyricum Akkermansia muciniphila This discrepancy has created confusion among consumers attempting to verify what SlimTide actually contains. Slim Tide Ingredients Explained Slim Tide is marketed as a weight-management and metabolism-support supplement. Different versions of the product online list different formulas, but several ingredients repeatedly appear across promotional websites. Below is a detailed explanation of the most commonly mentioned Slim Tide ingredients and their potential benefits. 1. Chicory Root Inulin What It Is Chicory root inulin is a natural prebiotic fiber extracted from the chicory plant. It is commonly used to support digestive health and gut bacteria . How It May Help Feeds beneficial gut bacteria Supports digestion May improve satiety (feeling full) Helps regulate bowel movements Can support blood sugar balance Scientific Interest Inulin is widely studied for gut microbiome support because it encourages the growth of beneficial bacteria like Bifidobacteria . 2. Resistant Starch What It Is Resistant starch is a carbohydrate that resists digestion in the small intestine and acts similarly to fiber. How It May Help Supports healthy digestion Promotes fullness May help reduce appetite Supports healthy gut bacteria May improve insulin sensitivity Scientific Interest Resistant starch is fermented in the colon, producing short-chain fatty acids that may support metabolic health. Common Sources Potatoes Green bananas Legumes Whole grains 3. Akkermansia muciniphila What It Is Akkermansia muciniphila is a beneficial gut bacterium receiving major attention in obesity and metabolic research. How It May Help Research suggests it may: Support metabolism Improve gut barrier health Help regulate body weight Reduce inflammation Support insulin sensitivity 4. Bifidobacterium infantis What It Is This is a probiotic bacterium naturally found in the digestive system. How It May Help Supports digestion May reduce bloating Helps maintain healthy gut bacteria Supports immune function 5. Clostridium butyricum What It Is A probiotic bacterium known for producing butyrate, a beneficial short-chain fatty acid. How It May Help Supports gut lining health Helps digestion May reduce inflammation Supports microbiome diversity 6. Berberine What It Is Berberine is a plant compound found in several herbs including barberry and goldenseal. How It May Help Research suggests berberine may: Support blood sugar control Improve ... Full story available on Benzinga.com
